gauge.name: Fishing Creek at Highway 5 below York

impacts.statement: Major Flooding continues.  Inundation of the Fishing Creek bridge on SC 5 is beginning, forcing closure.  The bottom of the bridge deck is reached at 22.898 ft.

Major Flooding continues.  Fishing Creek is approaching the SC 5 bridge, possibly forcing closure.  Fishing Creek is approaching the radar sensor at the USGS gaging station, disabling stage measurements.  The Fishing Creek bridge on Old York Rd is likely being overtopped.  Significant flooding of Holland Rd is likely.
Major Flood Stage.  Fishing Creek is approaching the SC 5 bridge.  Extensive and significant flooding of adjacent farmland is ongoing.  Significant structural and road impacts are likely.
Moderate Flooding continues.  Fishing Creek is approaching the bottom of the Old York Rd bridge.  Closure is imminent.
Minor Flood Stage.  Significant farmland flooding is beginning.  This level approximately represents the 10% annual chance of exceedance.
Action/Advisory Stage.  Fishing Creek is beginning to exceeds its banks, causing nuisance flooding of adjacent farmland, woodland, and other low-lying areas.

dataAttribution.text: USGS--Water Resources of the United States

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are not available. Only observed stages are available for this point.
